summary.text: Historical data is provided by S&P Global Market Intelligence. Real-time stock price data is provided by CBOE and 15-minute delayed consolidated tape pricing data is provided by Nasdaq UTP. Several fund data points are also provided by Finnhub.

overview.description: The Columbia EM Core ex-China ETF (XCEM) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in total market equity. The fund tracks a market-cap-weighted index that provides broad equity exposure to emerging markets excluding China. XCEM was launched on Sep 2, 2015 and is issued by Columbia Threadneedle.

data.title: Emerging Market Funds That Exclude China Are Beating the Index
data.text: Ex-China funds are attracting assets as geopolitical tensions with the U.S. grab headlines and China underperforms other emerging market countries.

data.title: 5 Emerging Market ETFs That Limit China Investments

holdings.infoBox: XCEM is an equity ETF with a total of 472 individual holdings. The top holdings are Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company stock at 15.98%, Samsung Electronics at 8.19%, SK hynix at 3.51%, Delta Electronics at 1.52%, and MediaTek at 1.43%.
